description: A popular cultural and creative park that combines traditional architecture with modern cultural creativity, making it a must-visit spot.
An important Mazu worship site along the southeastern coast of China, featuring ancient architecture and thriving incense offerings.
A Ming Dynasty garrison town preserving ancient city walls and traditional streets, with a serene and charming night view.
The ruins of the southern gate of the Song-Yuan ancient city, witnessing the historical changes of Quanzhou.
A modern coastal leisure area in Quanzhou, ideal for strolling, relaxing, and enjoying sea views.
Showcasing the historical and cultural ties between Fujian and Taiwan, offering insights into the development of cross-strait exchanges.
A coastal area with an extensive shoreline and a seaside boardwalk, a premium seaside leisure destination near Quanzhou.
The most historically rich ancient street in Quanzhou, featuring well-preserved Minnan architectural styles and a variety of local delicacies.
A famous commercial pedestrian street in Quanzhou, with a night market offering a wide range of local specialty snacks.
A Taoist temple built by the sea, dedicated to Zhenwu Emperor, an excellent spot for sea-viewing and blessings, with beautiful coastal scenery.
The ruins of an ancient city gate from the Song-Yuan period, reflecting the historical development of Quanzhou's ancient city, with high archaeological value.
Well-preserved alleys of traditional Minnan residences, showcasing the lifestyle of Quanzhou's ancient city.
An exemplary Minnan red-brick mansion, a masterpiece of Qing Dynasty overseas Chinese architectural art.
One of the oldest surviving Islamic mosques in China, reflecting the multicultural integration of Quanzhou.
The longest ancient beam-style stone bridge in China, renowned as 'No bridge under heaven is longer than this one,' a marvel of ancient engineering.
